LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING RE: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY RECEIVER SHOULD NOT BE APPOINTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT A RECEIVER SHALL NOT BE APPOINTED AT THIS TIME. ON JANUARY 27, 2014, THE COURT GRANTED DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S DECEMBER 18, 2013 ORDER, IN WHICH DEFENDANT'S TRANSFER OF HIS INTEREST IN 547 23RD AVENUE LLC ("THE LLC") WAS SET ASIDE. THE JANUARY 27, 2014 ORDER CHARGES DEFENDANT'S INTEREST IN THE LLC, IF HE HAS ANY. HOWEVER, DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO CHARGE HIS INTEREST IN THE LLC AND DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION TO THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY RECEIVER SHOULD NOT BE APPOINTED STATE THAT DEFENDANT NO LONGER HAS ANY INTEREST IN THE LLC BECAUSE HE TRANSFERRED ALL OF HIS SHARES TO HIS RELATIVES. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT OFFERED EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING THAT DEFENDANT CURRENTLY HAS AN INTEREST IN THE LLC. THEREFORE, A RECEIVER CANNOT BE APPOINTED TO MANAGE AN LLC THAT DEFENDANT CURRENTLY HAS NO INTEREST IN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Order To Show Cause Why Receiver Should Not Be Appointed
Off Calendar Jan-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DUY THAI, ESQ., 415.296.9927, FOR PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI; No other appearances.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling re: Order To Show Cause Why Receiver Should Not Be Appointed. The Court finds that a receiver shall not be appointed at this time. On January 27, 2014, the Court granted Defendant's motion for reconsideration of the Court's December 18, 2013 order, in which Defendant's transfer of his interest in 547 23rd Avenue LLC ("the LLC") was set aside. The January 27, 2014 Order charges Defendant's interest in the LLC, if he has any. However, Defendant's opposition to the motion to charge his interest in the LLC and Defendant's opposition to the order to show cause why receiver should not be appointed state that Defendant no longer has any interest in the LLC because he transferred all of his shares to his relatives. Plaintiff has not offered evidence demonstrating that Defendant currently has an interest in the LLC. Therefore, a receiver cannot be appointed to manage an LLC that Defendant currently has no interest in. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Charge Def'S Limited Liability Company Interest
Off Calendar Dec-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DUY THAI, ESQ., 415.296.9927, FOR PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI.
MARK LAPHAM, ESQ., 925.837.9007, FOR DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling WITH MODIFICATION: PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI'S Motion To Charge Defendant's Limited Liability Company Interest IS Granted. Pursuant to Civil Code 3439.07(c), the transfer of 547 23rd Avenue LLC from Demas Yan to Thai Ming Chiu, Kaman Liu, and Tina Yan is set aside. Defendant's interest in 547 23rd Avenue LLC (the "LLC") shall be charged for satisfaction of the unpaid balance of the Second Amended Judgment entered on November 13, 2013. Plaintiff has met his burden of establishing fraudulent intent. (Reddy v. Gonzalez (1992) 8 Cal.App.4th 118, 123.) Plaintiff has provided evidence showing several of the factors establishing fraudulent intent set forth in Civ. Code 3439.04(b) are met here, including, (1) transfer to an insider, (4) transfer was made after the debtor had been sued, (5) the transfer was of substantially all the debtor's assets, and (9) the debtor was insolvent shortly after the transfer was made. Plaintiff has also provided evidence showing that (8) the value of the consideration received by the debtor was not reasonably equivalent to the value of the asset transferred. Pursuant to the Order to Continue Debtor's Examination of Defendant Demas Yan, filed July 16, 2013 and signed by Judge Sullivan, Defendant Demas Yan's failure to produce the documents required by that order on or before July 24, 2013 bars their use in this proceeding with Charles Li. (See July 16, 2013 Order at 6-8 (requiring, inter alia, production of all documents relating to calculation of the $696,791 consideration recited in Yan's assignment of his interest in the LLC, documents relating to any consideration Yan received in exchange for assigning his interest in the LLC, and all documents evidencing debts or obligations forgiven or discharged as consideration for Yan's assignment of his interest in the LLC).) Even if the documents were considered, they are insufficient to establish the validity of the transfer. It is undisputed that the transfer of interests in the LLC was to three of Defendant's relatives. When a transfer is made to a relative, "the parties are held to a fuller and stricter proof of the consideration, and of the fairness of the transaction." (Johnson v. Drew (1963) 218 Cal.App.2d 614, 621.) Defendant has not met the standard of fuller and stricter proof to refute Plaintiff's evidence.B (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
